name: srdcheck
description: Deterministic rules verdicts over the SRD 5.2.1 for game-running agents. Exit codes are the verdict: 0 legal, 1 illegal, 2 cannot-adjudicate (unknown content or GM discretion — an honest refusal, not an error). Every verdict carries citations to the source text.
status: pre-release; query surface is unstable until v0.1

subcommands.jurisdiction <name>: Is this spell/creature/condition/action known to the loaded rulesets? exit 0 = known content (categories in payload); exit 2 = unknown/third-party content.
subcommands.query <type> <params-json>: Adjudicate a structured proposal. Current types: turn.plan (judge a whole proposed turn), turn.options (enumerate what remains legal), reaction.available, roll.compose (net advantage/disadvantage from source lists), attack.modifiers (compose an attack roll's modifiers from both combatants' conditions + distance; exhaustion reported as a flat modifier), mage-hand.use.
subcommands.--pipe: Read one {"type", "params"} JSON query from stdin.
subcommands.--schema: Print JSON Schema for input and output.

output: JSON verdict object on stdout: {verdict, exit_code, why, citations[], rule_ids[], adapter}
notes: The 'why' field is templated from cited rule text, not model-generated. Verdicts are deterministic: same query, same answer, every time. turn.options and turn.plan are consistency-tested against each other: everything enumerated is accepted, everything past a boundary is rejected.
